description: Fix icon validation gaps in plugin-tools and incorrect port display in CLI config show, found during backend code audit.

userStories.title: Support 8-digit and 5-digit hex colors in icon validation and grayscale conversion
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the inactive icon validation and grayscale conversion to correctly handle #RRGGBBAA and #RGBA hex color formats so that saturated colors in these formats are detected and converted to grayscale.
acceptanceCriteria: parseHex handles 5-character hex strings (#RGBA) by parsing the first 3 hex digits as RGB and ignoring the alpha digit
parseHex handles 9-character hex strings (#RRGGBBAA) by parsing the first 6 hex digits as RGB and ignoring the alpha pair
validateInactiveIconColors rejects SVGs containing saturated #RRGGBBAA colors (e.g., #FF000080)
generateInactiveIcon converts #RRGGBBAA colors to grayscale (preserving the alpha component)
generateInactiveIcon converts #RGBA colors to grayscale (preserving the alpha component)
convertColorToGray for hex colors preserves any alpha suffix when outputting the grayscale hex
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.ts

Three functions need changes:

1. `parseHex` (lines 129-149): Add handling for 5-char (#RGBA) and 9-char (#RRGGBBAA) hex strings. For 5-char, parse chars 1-3 as doubled hex pairs (same as 4-char but with extra alpha). For 9-char, parse chars 1-6 as hex pairs (same as 7-char but with extra alpha pair). Return only [R, G, B] — alpha is not needed for saturation/luminance checks.

2. `convertColorToGray` (around line 351-410): The hex branch currently just does `grayToHex(gray)` which outputs `#RRGGBB`. For #RRGGBBAA input, the alpha pair (last 2 chars) should be preserved. For #RGBA input, the alpha digit should be preserved. Check the input length and append the alpha portion after the grayscale hex. E.g., `#FF000080` → `#4C4C4C80`.

3. `isAchromaticColor` (around line 225-250): This already delegates to `parseColor` → `parseHex`, so once parseHex handles the new lengths, achromatic detection will work automatically.

Note: The `parseColor` function (line 193) calls `parseHex` for values starting with `#`, so fixing `parseHex` is the primary change. But `convertColorToGray` needs to preserve the alpha suffix.

userStories.title: Fix 'config show --show-secret' to resolve port from environment and flag
userStories.description: As a user running 'opentabs config show --show-secret', I want the printed MCP client config to show the correct port even when the server was started with --port or OPENTABS_PORT, so that I can copy-paste the config into my MCP client.
acceptanceCriteria: The port used in the MCP client config URLs is resolved from OPENTABS_PORT environment variable first, then config.port, then DEFAULT_PORT as fallback
If OPENTABS_PORT is set and is a valid port number, it takes precedence over config.port
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, lines 166-173.

Currently:
```ts
const port = typeof config.port === 'number' ? config.port : DEFAULT_PORT;
```

Should check OPENTABS_PORT env var first (same logic used in platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts and platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts for port resolution):
```ts
const envPort = process.env['OPENTABS_PORT'];
const port = envPort ? Number(envPort) : (typeof config.port === 'number' ? config.port : DEFAULT_PORT);
```

Check platform/cli/src/parse-port.ts — the `parsePort` function may be usable here for consistent validation, but it's designed for commander option parsing. A simpler inline check is appropriate.

Note: the --port CLI flag cannot be reflected here since `config show` has no way to know what flag `start` was run with. But OPENTABS_PORT is a persistent environment variable that can be read.
